name: Tortosa to Deltebre by gravel bike
name_original: De Tortosa a Deltebre en bici de gravel
description:
We invite you to accompany the majestic Ebro on its final stretch to the sea. This Route enters the network of gravel tracks and irrigation canals that extends between the rice fields and wetlands of Deltebre.

The route is circular from the Bikefriendly SB Corona de Tortosa hotel (https://sbhotelsandbike.sbhotels.es/). This small city deserves a walk through the center, either on the way out or on the way back, to enjoy its architecture, charm and gastronomy. Between Tortosa and Amposta you get close to the banks of the Ebro, where you can pedal at a good pace as you enjoy the breeze and the landscape. Along the way you will find numerous lagoons ideal for contemplating birdlife.

Despite the distance, this Route is not too demanding. However, it is recommended that you bring enough water and food, as well as some spare parts and tools that will allow you to get out of any unforeseen event.

Any sunny day from autumn to spring is perfect for cycling along the Ebro Delta. At this time of year, temperatures are mild thanks to the many sunny days at the mouth of the river. In summer, it is also possible to do the route, but not during the central hours of the day, but rather early or late in the day, when you will enjoy enviable light.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Tortosa offers diverse terrain, transitioning from the flat expanse of the Ebro Delta to the mountainous landscapes of Els Ports Natural Park. The region features a network of forest tracks, rural paths, and repurposed greenways, providing varied experiences for gravel cyclists. Riders can explore the Ebro River and its delta, characterized by rice paddies and lagoons, or ascend into the rugged limestone peaks reaching over 1,400 meters. This geographical diversity makes Tortosa a varied destination for gravel biking. Best gravel bike trails around Tortosa The most popular gravel bike trail is Tortosa to Deltebre by gravel bike, a 57.5 miles (92.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route traverses the flat, distinctive landscapes of the Ebro Delta. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Camí natural de l'Ebre (Ebro Natural Path), a difficult 58.2 miles (93.6 km) path. This trail follows the Ebro River, offering scenic views along its banks. Local gravel bikers also love the View of the Ebro Delta – View of the Ebro River loop from Tortosa, a 69.6 miles (112.0 km) trail leading through the Ebro Delta and along the Ebro River, often completed in about 7 hours 58 minutes. Gravel biking around Tortosa is defined by the flat, ecological richness of the Ebro Delta, the challenging mountainous terrain of Els Ports Natural Park, and accessible greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Tortosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 20 reviews. More than 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Tortosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Tortosa?
A: Gravel biking around Tortosa offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find everything from the flat, expansive landscapes of the Ebro Delta, characterized by rice paddies and lagoons, to the rugged, mountainous paths of the Els Ports Natural Park. Many routes combine forest tracks, rural paths, and repurposed greenways (Vías Verdes), providing a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there gravel bike routes suitable for beginners or families in Tortosa?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are options for moderate fitness levels and families. The region's Greenways, such as sections of the Val de Zafán Via Verde and the Baix Ebre Greenway, offer well-maintained, relatively flat surfaces perfect for a smoother ride. These paths often pass through scenic areas like pine forests and olive groves, with tunnels and viaducts adding to the experience.

Q: What natural features or wildlife can I see while gravel biking in the Ebro Delta?
A: The Ebro Delta is a wetland area of immense ecological importance. While gravel biking here, you can expect to see vast rice paddies, reed beds, and lagoons. It's a haven for thousands of birds, including exotic flamingos, particularly in protected natural spaces like La Tancada and La Encanyissada lagoons. You might also encounter the Ullals de Baltasar, which are natural freshwater springs.

Q: Are there any challenging gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes extending into the Parc Natural dels Ports de Tortosa-Beseit offer a rugged and mountainous experience with significant elevation gains. These routes can include ascents to peaks like Mont Caro, the highest point of the Els Ports Massif at 1,447 meters, providing panoramic views of the Ebro Delta.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the gravel bike trails near Tortosa?
A: Many routes incorporate historical and natural landmarks. In the Ebro Delta, you might cross the historic Amposta Suspension Bridge or the modern Pont Lo Passador. For those exploring the mountainous areas, the Parc Natural dels Ports de Tortosa-Beseit features diverse landscapes, including the Fageda del Retaule, Europe's southernmost beech forest. You can also find attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/692374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4567270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortifications of Punta de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Tortosa?
A: The region is ideal for exploration, especially during spring and autumn. These seasons typically offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable gravel biking experience across both the delta and mountainous terrains.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Tortosa?
A: There are over 40 gravel bike trails around Tortosa, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 21 moderate routes and 20 difficult routes, ensuring options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Can I find loop routes for gravel biking in Tortosa?
A: Yes, there are several excellent loop routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro Delta – View of the Ebro River loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 112 km ride. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lower Ebro Greenway – View of the Ebro River loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 41.7 km.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Tortosa?
A: The gravel biking routes in Tortosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Over 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique blend of flat delta landscapes and challenging mountainous sections.

Q: Are there any gorges or canyons accessible from the gravel bike trails?
A: While not directly on the gravel trails, the wider Tortosa area is known for its stunning gorges. You can explore nearby natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular for hiking and offer breathtaking scenery.

Q: What are the main differences between gravel biking in the Ebro Delta and Els Ports Natural Park?
A: Gravel biking in the Ebro Delta is characterized by its flat terrain, extensive rice paddies, and opportunities for birdwatching, offering a unique, often less strenuous experience. In contrast, Els Ports Natural Park provides a rugged, mountainous environment with significant elevation gains, technical sections, and panoramic views, catering to more challenging and adventurous rides.
tips: Former railway bridge over the Ebro and direct entry into Tortosa. Excellent panoramic view of the river and the city.
A true symbol of the city, the Amposta Bridge was built in 1915 to connect the towns of the Ebro Delta. It is an architectural and engineering gem!

Located in an unparalleled setting, the outcrops of Baltassar are freshwater springs, surrounded by flora dominated by eucalyptus. The landscape is unique, as well as the wildlife. If you're on a road bike, you might have to push it for a few moments, but it's definitely worth the trip!
The Ebro delta is full of gravel tracks that stretch between irrigation canals and crop fields. This typical landscape is ideal for letting yourself be carried away by pedal stroke.
There is an excellent viewpoint to see the lagoon and the birds that live there. Around there are a couple of restaurants and bars if necessary.
Views of the old defense tower built by order of Felipe II due to attacks by Saracen piracy. Today only the half-demolished walls of the wall remain visible.

La Tancada is the lagoon with the highest density of flamingos in the entire Delta. At the viewpoint there is space for 15 or 20 people without problems. From it you can take good photographs of the birds.

Very picturesque and when it's hot over the delta, the residents are happy about the beautiful trees. There is no altitude here but the heat on the plains can be exhausting. Make sure you have enough water with you so you don't have to drink from the Ebro or the rice fields 😄
This bridge has a bike lane, pedestrian walkway and benches perfect for admiring the Ebro and its estuary.

The SB Corona Tortosa is one of the Bikefriendly hotels with the best facilities in the network of accommodations for cyclists. It has a modern and well-kept space exclusively for storing guests' bicycles. Only cyclists have the key to this space and, in addition, each bike rack has its own individual lock. In the same area they have a bicycle repair and washing area with tools and, best of all, a washing machine and dryer available to cyclists. 

The accommodation itself offers a mountain bike rental service and organizes the Gran Fondo Terres de l'Ebre annually. https://bikefriendly.bike/hoteles/sb-corona-de-tortosa/
